seo.meta_title: WorkOS vs Supabase Auth for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins? (Updated Week 24, 2026)
seo.meta_description: I compared WorkOS and Supabase Auth head-to-head for Next.js 2026. Pricing: Custom pricing (enterprise) vs Free – $75/mo. DX scores: 5/10 vs 7/10. Read on for the verdict.
seo.og_title: WorkOS vs Supabase Auth — Which Auth Tool Ships Faster?
seo.og_description: Choosing auth for your Next.js app? WorkOS and Supabase Auth compared: Custom pricing (enterprise) vs Free – $75/mo, 45 minutes vs 15 minutes setup.

hero.headline: WorkOS vs Supabase Auth for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of WorkOS and Supabase Auth — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Most Next.js teams I've talked to end up choosing Supabase Auth over WorkOS for one reason: Supabase Auth just feels built for App Router. That 7/10 score reflects real middleware and server component support. WorkOS isn't bad  –  but it's playing catch-up on DX.
verdict_text: Supabase Auth is the better fit for Next.js App Router projects. Here's why: the 7/10 score translates to less code, fewer workarounds, and faster middleware setup. WorkOS is still worth evaluating if no free tier (enterprise sales) is a deciding factor  –  just know you'll trade some DX for the savings.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t2: Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier

pros: Enterprise-grade SSO with SAML/OIDC support out of the box
SCIM provisioning for enterprise directory integration
SOC 2 compliant and enterprise-ready from day one
Clean API design with excellent documentation
Used by Vercel, Airbnb, and other top-tier tech companies
cons: No free tier - requires sales conversation to get started
Not designed for consumer auth or B2C applications
Pricing is opaque and requires a sales call
Overkill for teams that don't need enterprise SSO

pros: Extremely generous free tier with 50,000 monthly active users
Integrated with Supabase database, storage, and realtime subscriptions
Built-in support for email, phone, OAuth, and magic links
cons: Tightly coupled to the Supabase PostgreSQL ecosystem
Less focused on auth-only use cases - it's part of a larger platform
Organization and team management features are less mature than Clerk
RLS policies can become complex to manage at scale

faq.question: Should I choose WorkOS or Supabase Auth for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. WorkOS scores 5/10 for Next.js integration with a Advanced learning curve. Supabase Auth scores 7/10 with a Easy learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Supabase Auth because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if No free tier (enterprise sales) or Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is WorkOS or Supabase Auth more affordable?
faq.answer: WorkOS starts at Custom pricing (enterprise) with a free tier of No free tier (enterprise sales). Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo with a free tier of Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Supabase Auth offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is WorkOS or Supabase Auth easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve with an estimated setup time of 45 minutes. Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. Supabase Auth is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use WorkOS and Supabase Auth together?
faq.answer: Using both WorkOS and Supabase Auth in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: WorkOS or Supabase Auth?
faq.answer: WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 5/10, and a user rating of 4★. Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve, 7/10, and 4.4★. On paper, Supabase Auth leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
